testPlan.name: claude-query Test Plan
testPlan.author: Sola Samuel / Spearhead Finance Ltd
testPlan.overview: Verifies claude-query against its product backlog. Each test case links to a backlog story, feature, or epic via the `traces` field. The plan emphasises the two hard requirements: destructive queries must never execute, and --schema-only must never transmit row data.

inScope: CLI argument parsing and flag validation
Claude tool-use request/response handling (mocked Claude where appropriate)
All five adapters: schema extraction and query execution
Query modes: dry-run, schema-only, explain, limit
Output formatters: table, json, csv, markdown

environments: Notion test database and Airtable test base
Mocked Claude API for deterministic tool-use responses; live Claude for smoke e2e
entryCriteria: Adapter under test builds and connects.
exitCriteria: No open Critical or High defect on a safety case.
Backlog traceability complete: every P0 story has at least one linked test case.

testCases.title: claude-query exits with error when --source is omitted
testCases.expected: Process exits non-zero with a clear 'missing --source' error message.

testCases.title: Positional question is captured and passed downstream
steps: Run with a question and a valid --source against a stub adapter
testCases.expected: The question string reaches the Claude request builder unchanged.

testCases.title: --output rejects unsupported values
testCases.expected: Process exits non-zero; only table|json|csv|markdown accepted.

testCases.title: --limit defaults to 100 and parses as integer
testCases.expected: Default resolves to 100; explicit value parses to integer 25; non-numeric is rejected.

testCases.title: System prompt contains schema text and SELECT-only instruction, no raw rows
steps: Build the system prompt for a Postgres source
testCases.expected: Prompt includes formatted schema and the SELECT-only instruction; contains no raw row data.

testCases.title: execute_query tool definition requires query, query_type, reasoning
testCases.expected: required = [query, query_type, reasoning]; query_type enum = [sql, notion_filter, airtable_formula, json_path].

testCases.title: tool_use response block is parsed and query extracted
preconditions: Mocked Claude returning a tool_use block
testCases.expected: query and reasoning are extracted from the execute_query tool_use block.

testCases.title: Response with no tool_use block produces a clear error
testCases.expected: A clear error is surfaced; nothing is executed.

testCases.title: Adapters conform to DataSourceAdapter interface
steps: Type-check / assert each adapter exposes name, connect, getSchema, executeQuery, disconnect
testCases.expected: Every adapter satisfies the interface; getSchema returns SchemaContext; executeQuery returns Row[].

testCases.title: Postgres schema extraction: tables, columns/types, FKs
preconditions: Seed Postgres with customers/orders and an FK
testCases.expected: All public tables, each column name/type, and the customers→orders FK are present.

testCases.title: Postgres schema: row counts and 3 sample distinct values, TABLESAMPLE on large tables
testCases.expected: Row count present; up to 3 distinct samples per column; large table uses TABLESAMPLE SYSTEM (no full scan).

testCases.title: Validated SELECT executes and returns Row[]; connection closes on disconnect
testCases.expected: Rows returned as Row[]; pool/connection is released on disconnect.

testCases.title: CSV type inference with min/max and 5 samples
testCases.expected: Types are string/number/date/boolean; min/max on numeric & date; row count and 5 distinct samples per column.

testCases.title: CSV in-memory filter via json_path
preconditions: mocked Claude returning a json_path filter
testCases.expected: Filter is applied to the parsed array; only matching rows returned as Row[].

testCases.title: JSON schema inference for flat and nested with dot-notation
preconditions: Fixture flat JSON and nested JSON
testCases.expected: Schema inferred from first 10 records; nested paths flattened with dot notation.

testCases.title: JSON json_path filter resolves nested paths
steps: Run a query on a nested field
testCases.expected: Dot-notation path resolves; matching records returned.

testCases.title: Notion property schema includes select option values
preconditions: Notion test database with a select property
testCases.expected: Property names/types and all select/multi-select option values captured.

testCases.title: Notion filter object passed to databases.query()
testCases.expected: The filter/sorts object is passed through unchanged; pages mapped to Row[].

testCases.title: Airtable field schema includes linked tables and select options
preconditions: Airtable test base with a linked field
testCases.expected: Field names/types, linked table names, and select options captured.

testCases.title: Airtable filterByFormula applied
steps: Capture filterByFormula passed to the SDK
testCases.expected: The formula string is passed as filterByFormula; records mapped to Row[].

testCases.title: SAFETY: non-SELECT statements are rejected before execution
steps: Validate each of INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, DROP, ALTER, CREATE, TRUNCATE
testCases.expected: All non-SELECT statements throw a clear error; the SELECT passes. No execution occurs for rejected statements.

testCases.title: SAFETY: stacked / obfuscated statements rejected
steps: Validate `SELECT * FROM t -- ; DROP TABLE t`
testCases.expected: Stacked statements, comment-obfuscated DDL, and parse failures all result in rejection, never execution.

testCases.title: System prompt forbids non-SELECT statements (soft gate)
steps: Build the system prompt for a SQL source
testCases.expected: Prompt explicitly names the forbidden statement types and is present for every SQL-source request.

testCases.title: SAFETY: --schema-only swaps to describe_query and never executes
testCases.expected: describe_query tool is sent; Claude returns the would-be query plus explanation; adapter.executeQuery is never called.

testCases.title: SAFETY: schema-only payload contains no row data
preconditions: Seed Postgres with recognisable sentinel row values
steps: Capture the full outbound payload to Claude
testCases.expected: No sentinel row values appear anywhere in the payload; mode is reflected in console output.

testCases.title: --dry-run prints query and does not execute
testCases.expected: Constructed query is printed; executeQuery is never called; clean exit with no results table.

testCases.title: --explain prints reasoning before results
testCases.expected: Reasoning is printed before the results; without --explain it is captured but not printed.

testCases.title: --limit caps and indicates truncation
preconditions: Result set larger than the limit
testCases.expected: Exactly 5 rows shown; truncation is indicated to the user; default of 100 applies when omitted.

testCases.title: Table output renders and handles empty sets
testCases.expected: cli-table3 renders headers/rows by default; empty sets show a clear 'no rows' message.

testCases.title: JSON output is a valid parseable array
testCases.expected: Output is a valid JSON array of objects with no table formatting.

testCases.title: CSV output has headers and escapes special characters
testCases.expected: Header row present; commas/quotes escaped; row count matches result (after limit).

testCases.title: Markdown output is a valid GFM table
testCases.expected: Valid GFM table with header separator/alignment row; renders correctly when pasted.

testCases.title: SchemaContext serialised to readable text, not JSON
steps: Serialise it for the system prompt
testCases.expected: Output is human-readable text (not JSON), representing tables, columns, types, relationships, via a shared renderer.

testCases.title: Schema cached by connection hash; cache hit skips re-extraction
steps: Run two queries in one session
testCases.expected: Second query reuses the cache (keyed by connection-string hash); getSchema runs once.

testCases.title: Relevant-table selection sends only named tables in detail
testCases.expected: First call sends full schema; subsequent call sends only the named tables in detail; small schemas degrade gracefully.

testCases.title: Last 10 queries recalled in session
testCases.expected: Up to 10 most-recent queries listed (question + constructed query), most-recent-first.

testCases.title: History persisted to ~/.claude-query/history.json; malformed file tolerated
testCases.expected: History written to the path (dir created if missing); malformed file is handled without crashing.

testCases.title: REPL accepts multiple questions and reuses schema cache
testCases.expected: Both questions answered; schema extracted once; exit command ends the session cleanly.

testCases.title: --save exports results in the chosen format
testCases.expected: Results written to out.csv in CSV format; success path reported; existing-file behaviour is defined.

testCases.title: README documents install, API key, per-adapter setup, flags, governance, limitations
testCases.expected: Covers npx + global install, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, per-adapter connection formats with 5 examples each, full flag reference, --schema-only governance note, and known limitations.

testCases.title: End-to-end Postgres smoke: question → constructed query → results table
steps: Run: claude-query --source postgres://localhost/customers "Which customers signed up in the last 30 days and have not made a purchase?"
testCases.expected: Constructed SELECT is shown, passes safety validation, executes, and a results table is rendered.

traceabilityMatrix.note: Every P0 backlog story maps to at least one P0 test case. Safety stories (STORY-3.1.1/3.1.2/3.3.1/3.3.2) are covered by security-type cases TC-021, TC-022, TC-024, TC-025.
